title: shorea macroptera subsp. baillonii
intro: Shorea macroptera subsp. baillonii, also known as the Borneo ironwood, is a subspecies of tree native to the rainforests of Borneo, characterized by its large trunk and dense foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Shorea macroptera subsp. baillonii are believed to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the tree's heartwood is often used to make medicine, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
